rulings.Case Title: SASCO, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. BOVIS LEND LEASE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 19, 2008, line 9, DEFENDANT BOVIS LEND LEASE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF PLEADS A LOSS OCCASIONED BY DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT. ALTHOUGH THE AMOUNT OF THE LOSS MAY BE UNCERTAIN AT THIS TIME, THAT IS NOT A BASIS FOR SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MAY TSAI et al VS. CONNIE FUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 19, 2008, line 10, DEFENDANTS CONNIE FUNG, RONALD FUNG, FUNG TRUST DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CENTRAL TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS THE CONSTRUCTION OF THE GARAGE ANDTHE RESULTING DAMAGE AS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT AND PLAINTIFFS' RENT BOARD PETITIONS. THESE CLAIMS ARE GOVERNED BY CCP SECTION 340(a) AND THE HOLDING IN MENEFEE V. OSTAWARI (1991) 228CAL.APP.3D 239 AND ARE BARRED BY THE APPLICABLE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. IT MAY BE THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE CLAIMS THAT ARE UNRELATED AND PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD SUCH CLAIMS AND TO SET FORTH FACTS THAT REFLECT THAT THE AMENDED CLAIMS DO NOT ARISE FROM THE BARRED CLAIMS. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: WADE ANTHONY ROBERTSON VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 19, 2008, line 12, PETITIONER WADE ROBERTSON'S Notice Of Hearing Re: Writ Of Administrative Mandamus. THE WRIT IS DENIED. OFFICER RYAN HAD A REASONABLE BASIS TO EFFECT THE STOP. THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE'S ANALYSIS IS CONSISTENT WITH THE EVIDENCE AND CONSISTENT WITH THE RESPONSIBILITY OF THE TRIER OF FACT TO REVIEW AND WEIGH THE EVIDENCE. THERE WAS NO BASIS FOR PETITIONER TO REFUSE TO TAKE A DUI TEST. ANY DELAY ASSOCIATED WITH THE PRODUCTION IS NOT A BASIS FOR OVERTURNING THE DECISION. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 19, 2008, line 21, DEFENDANTS LA-NI LITHMAN AND MIKE SKIPPER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED.
